page: Amazon Web Services i8g.large is a storage-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes a bundled 468 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage and operates on a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1707.28 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1813.0, both falling into the average category. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform strongly, with gzip compression reaching 30.29 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.15 GB/sec. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is weak due to the low core count, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3402.28 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3235.0. Memory bandwidth is average for large uncached reads at 56.64 GB/sec but weak for writes at 22.82 GB/sec. Database operations on Passmark score an average of 3025.4, while Redis SET operations achieve 4.7 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the i8g.large offers a dense storage-to-compute ratio, making it a viable option for workloads requiring fast local scratch space or caching without the overhead of high-core-count instances. The dedicated ARM64 cores provide predictable execution, though the lack of AVX or AMX extensions limits its utility for advanced vector math or heavy machine learning. This instance is best suited for lightweight database operations, local caching layers, single-threaded file compression tasks, and basic web serving where local NVMe storage is highly utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.large is a storage-optimized instance powered by a 2.8 GHz ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs. It features 16.0 GB of memory and a bundled 468 GB local NVMe SSD, running on the Nitro hypervisor with a 1 Gbps baseline network. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU capabilities and strong single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core and memory write bandwidth metrics are relatively weak. Lacking advanced vector extensions, this instance offers a qualitative balance of high local storage density and dedicated ARM resources. It is well-suited for lightweight database operations, caching, and single-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.large is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 468 GB local NVMe SSD for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.large is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance with 2 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 468 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, balanced memory, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for localized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor with 2 dedicated cores
Equipped with 16.0 GB of memory and a 468 GB local NVMe SSD
Strong single-threaded compression and average single-core benchmark performance
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight databases, caching, and local file processing
